timeline.title: Madurai’s Sacred Landscape in the Early Modern City
timeline.overview: This one-day route follows Madurai as a temple-centered city shaped by worship, pilgrimage, water, and royal-era urbanism. Beginning at the core of Meenakshi Temple, it expands to Azhagar Kovil to show the wider sacred geography, then closes at Vandiyur Mariamman Teppakulam, a N⁠
timeline.whyThisRouteWorks: The stops trace a coherent sacred landscape from the city’s defining temple core to an important regional pilgrimage shrine and then to a Nayak-period tank that still structures ritual life. Together they show how early modern Madurai linked worship, water, and urban form.
timeline.transitionLogic: Start at the city’s central monument, Meenakshi Temple, where the temple-city model is most visible. Move northwest to Azhagar Kovil to widen the lens from urban core to surrounding pilgrimage terrain. End at Vandiyur Mariamman Teppakulam,

chapters.summary: Madurai’s defining standing monument and the clearest expression of a temple-centered city. Its long rebuilding history across medieval and early modern centuries makes it the anchor of the urban sacred landscape.
A major standing Vishnu temple that extends the story beyond the Shaiva core. It shows how Madurai’s sacred geography reached beyond the city center into a wider pilgrimage network.
A surviving Nayak-period temple tank tied to the float-festival tradition. It preserves the water infrastructure that supported ritual life and the early modern city’s ceremonial rhythms.

feasibleStops.title: Meenakshi Amman Temple
feasibleStops.areaLabel: Core temple city, south of the Vaigai
feasibleStops.historicalSummary: Major surviving temple complex at the heart of Madurai’s sacred and urban identity; the present fabric reflects long building, rebuilding, and patronage across the medieval and early modern eras.
feasibleStops.whyItMatters: It is the defining standing monument of Madurai and anchors the city’s historic temple-centered urban form.

feasibleStops.title: Azhagar Kovil
feasibleStops.historicalSummary: Standing Vishnu temple associated with the wider Madurai sacred landscape, surviving as an important regional pilgrimage site outside the core city center.
feasibleStops.whyItMatters: It broadens the dossier beyond the Shaiva core and shows Madurai’s larger sacred geography.

feasibleStops.title: Vandiyur Mariamman Teppakulam
feasibleStops.historicalSummary: Large surviving temple tank associated with the Madurai ritual landscape and the float-festival tradition; built in the Nayak period and still used today.
feasibleStops.whyItMatters: It preserves the hydraulic and ritual infrastructure of historic Madurai, linking water management with festival life.
